heading: Definitions and special rules for purposes of minimum survivor annuity requirements
label: 417 Definitions and special rules for purposes of minimum survivor annuity requirements
notes: Amendments2014—Subsec. (e)(3)(D). substituted “if section 430(h)(2)(D)” for “if—Pub. L. 113–295“(i) section 430(h)(2)(D)”and “described in such section.” for “described in such section,” and struck out cls. (ii) and (iii) which applied by substituting “section 417(e)(3)(A)(ii)(II)” for “section 412(b)(5)(B)(ii)(II)” and listed the applicable percentage under section 430(h)(2)(G) for plan years beginning in 2008 to 2011.section 430(h)(2)(G)(i)(II) of this title2012—Subsec. (e)(3)(C), (D). substituted “section 430(h)(2)(C) (determined by not taking into account any adjustment under clause (iv) thereof)” for “section 430(h)(2)(C)”.Pub. L. 112–1412008—Subsec. (e)(3)(D)(i). substituted “subparagraph (C)” for “clause (ii)”.Pub. L. 110–4582006—Subsec. (a)(1)(A)(ii), (iii). , added cl. (ii) and redesignated former cl. (ii) as (iii).Pub. L. 109–280, § 1004(a)(1)Subsec. (a)(3)(A)(i). , inserted “and of the qualified optional survivor annuity” before comma at end.Pub. L. 109–280, § 1004(a)(3)Subsec. (a)(6)(A). , substituted “180-day” for “90-day”.Pub. L. 109–280, § 1102(a)(1)(A)Subsec. (e)(3). , reenacted heading without change and amended text of par. (3) generally, substituting provisions relating to determination of present value by using the applicable mortality table and the applicable interest rate, provisions defining “applicable mortality table” and “applicable interest rate”, and provisions relating to determination of the adjusted first, second, and third segment rates, for provisions relating to determination of present value, provisions defining “applicable mortality table” and “applicable interest rate”, and provisions stating exception for a distribution from a plan that was adopted and in effect before the date of the enactment of the Retirement Protection Act of 1994.Pub. L. 109–280, § 302(b)Subsec. (g). , added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 109–280, § 1004(a)(2)2002—Subsec. (e)(1). , substituted “exceed the amount that can be distributed without the participant’s consent under section 411(a)(11)” for “exceed the dollar limit under section 411(a)(11)(A)”.Pub. L. 107–147, § 411(r)(1)(A)Subsec. (e)(2)(A). , substituted “exceeds the amount that can be distributed without the participant’s consent under section 411(a)(11)” for “exceeds the dollar limit under section 411(a)(11)(A)”.Pub. L. 107–147, § 411(r)(1)(B)1997—Subsec. (e)(1), (2). substituted “dollar limit” for “$3,500” in headings of pars. (1) and (2) and “the dollar limit under section 411(a)(11)(A)” for “$3,500” in text of pars. (1) and (2)(A).Pub. L. 105–341996—Subsec. (a)(7). added par. (7).Pub. L. 104–1881994—Subsec. (e)(3). amended par. (3) generally, substituting present provisions for provisions directing that present value be calculated by using a rate no greater than the applicable interest rate or 120 percent of such rate, depending upon amount of vested accrued benefit, and defining “applicable interest rate”.Pub. L. 103–4651989—Subsec. (a)(3)(B)(ii). added sentence at end and struck out former subcl. (V) which read as follows: “A reasonable period after separation from service in case of a participant who separates before attaining age 35.”Pub. L. 101–2391988—Subsec. (e)(3)(A). substituted “clause (ii)” for “subclause (II)” in last sentence.Pub. L. 100–6471986—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “section 401(a)(11)” for “section 401(a)(ii)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(15)(A)Subsec. (a)(1)(B). , substituted “paragraphs (2), (3), and (4)” for “paragraphs (2) and (3)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(4)(A)(i)Subsec. (a)(2)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (A) read as follows: “the spouse of the participant consents in writing to such election, and the spouse’s consent acknowledges the effect of such election and is witnessed by a plan representative or a notary public, or”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(6)(A)Subsec. (a)(3)(B). , amended subpar. (B) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (B) read as follows: “Each plan shall provide to each participant, within the period beginning with the first day of the plan year in which the participant attains age 32 and ending with the close of the plan year preceding the plan year in which the participant attains age 35 (and consistent with such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe), a written explanation with respect to the qualified preretirement survivor annuity comparable to that required under subparagraph (A).”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(5)(A)Subsec. (a)(4). , added par. (4). Former par. (4) redesignated (5).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(4)(A)(ii)Subsec. (a)(5), (6). , (11)(A), redesignated former par. (4) as (5) and inserted in subpar. (A) “if such benefit may not be waived (or another beneficiary selected) and” before “if the plan”. Former par. (5) redesignated (6).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(4)(A)(ii)Subsec. (c)(1). , substituted “survivor annuity for the life of” for “survivor annuity or the life of”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(15)(B), inserted “In the case of an individual who separated from service before the date of such individual’s death, subparagraph (A)(ii)(I) shall not apply.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(1)(A)Subsec. (c)(2). , substituted “the portion of the account balance of the participant (as of the date of death) to which the participant had a nonforfeitable right (within the meaning of section 411(a))” for “the account balance of the participant as of the date of death”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(9)(A)(i)Subsec. (c)(3). , added par. (3).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(9)(A)(ii)Subsec. (e)(3). , amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) read as follows: “For purposes of paragraphs (1) and (2), the present value of a qualified joint and survivor annuity or a qualified preretirement survivor annuity shall be determined as of the date of the distribution and by using an interest rate not greater than the interest rate which would be used (as of the date of the distribution) by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation for purposes of determining the present value of a lump sum distribution on plan termination.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1139(b)Subsec. (f)(1). , substituted “such participant’s accrued benefit” for “the accrued benefit derived from employer contributions”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(8)(A)Subsec. (f)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “The term ‘annuity starting date’ means the first day of the first period for which an amount is received as an annuity (whether by reason of retirement or disability).”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(12)(A)Subsec. (f)(5). , added par. (5) and redesignated former par. (5) as (6).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(4)(A)(iii)Subsec. (f)(6), (7). , added par. (6) and redesignated former par. (6) as (7).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(10)(A), redesignated former par. (5) as (6).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1898(b)(4)(A)(iii)

children.heading: Election to waive qualified joint and survivor annuity or qualified preretirement survivor annuity
children.label: a Election to waive qualified joint and survivor annuity or qualified preretirement survivor annuity
i
children.text: may elect at any time during the applicable election period to waive the qualified joint and survivor annuity form of benefit or the qualified preretirement survivor annuity form of benefit (or both),

children.label: ii
children.text: if the participant elects a waiver under clause (i), may elect the qualified optional survivor annuity at any time during the applicable election period, and

children.label: iii
children.text: may revoke any such election at any time during the applicable election period, and

children.label: B
children.text: the plan meets the requirements of paragraphs (2), (3), and (4) of this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: the spouse of the participant consents in writing to such election, (ii) such election designates a beneficiary (or a form of benefits) which may not be changed without spousal consent (or the consent of the spouse expressly permits designations by the participant without any requirement of further consent by the spouse), and (iii) the spouse’s consent acknowledges the effect of such election and is witnessed by a plan representative or a notary public, or

children.label: B
children.text: it is established to the satisfaction of a plan representative that the consent required under subparagraph (A) may not be obtained because there is no spouse, because the spouse cannot be located, or because of such other circumstances as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Explanation of joint and survivor annuity
children.label: A Explanation of joint and survivor annuity
i
children.text: the terms and conditions of the qualified joint and survivor annuity and of the qualified optional survivor annuity,

children.label: ii
children.text: the participant’s right to make, and the effect of, an election under paragraph (1) to waive the joint and survivor annuity form of benefit,

children.label: iii
children.text: the rights of the participant’s spouse under paragraph (2), and

children.label: iv
children.text: the right to make, and the effect of, a revocation of an election under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Explanation of qualified preretirement survivor annuity
children.label: B Explanation of qualified preretirement survivor annuity

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each plan shall provide to each participant, within the applicable period with respect to such participant (and consistent with such regulations as the Secretary may prescribe), a written explanation with respect to the qualified preretirement survivor annuity comparable to that required under subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: The period beginning with the first day of the plan year in which the participant attains age 32 and ending with the close of the plan year preceding the plan year in which the participant attains age 35.

children.label: II
children.text: A reasonable period after the individual becomes a participant.

children.label: III
children.text: A reasonable period ending after paragraph (5) ceases to apply to the participant.

children.label: IV
children.text: A reasonable period ending after section 401(a)(11) applies to the participant.

children.heading: Requirement of spousal consent for using plan assets as security for loans
children.label: 4 Requirement of spousal consent for using plan assets as security for loans
A
children.text: the spouse of the participant (if any) consents in writing to such use during the 90-day period ending on the date on which the loan is to be so secured, and

children.label: B
children.text: requirements comparable to the requirements of paragraph (2) are met with respect to such consent.

children.heading: Special rules where plan fully subsidizes costs
children.label: 5 Special rules where plan fully subsidizes costs

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirements of this subsection shall not apply with respect to the qualified joint and survivor annuity form of benefit or the qualified preretirement survivor annuity form of benefit, as the case may be, if such benefit may not be waived (or another beneficiary selected) and if the plan fully subsidizes the costs of such benefit.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: B Definition
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a plan fully subsidizes the costs of a benefit if under the plan the failure to waive such benefit by a participant would not result in a decrease in any plan benefits with respect to such participant and would not result in increased contributions from such participant.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an election to waive the qualified joint and survivor annuity form of benefit, the 180-day period ending on the annuity starting date, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an election to waive the qualified preretirement survivor annuity, the period which begins on the first day of the plan year in which the participant attains age 35 and ends on the date of the participant’s death.

children.heading: Special rules relating to time for written explanation
children.label: 7 Special rules relating to time for written explanation

children.heading: Explanation may be provided after annuity starting date
children.label: A Explanation may be provided after annuity starting date

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A plan may provide the written explanation described in paragraph (3)(A) after the annuity starting date. In any case to which this subparagraph applies, the applicable election period under paragraph (6) shall not end before the 30th day after the date on which such explanation is provided.

children.heading: Regulatory authority
children.label: ii Regulatory authority
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations limit the application of clause (i), except that such regulations may not limit the period of time by which the annuity starting date precedes the provision of the written explanation other than by providing that the annuity starting date may not be earlier than termination of employment.

children.heading: Waiver of 30-day period
children.label: B Waiver of 30-day period
children.text: A plan may permit a participant to elect (with any applicable spousal consent) to waive any requirement that the written explanation be provided at least 30 days before the annuity starting date (or to waive the 30-day requirement under subparagraph (A)) if the distribution commences more than 7 days after such explanation is provided.

children.heading: Definition of qualified joint and survivor annuity
children.label: b Definition of qualified joint and survivor annuity

children.text: for the life of the participant with a survivor annuity for the life of the spouse which is not less than 50 percent of (and is not greater than 100 percent of) the amount of the annuity which is payable during the joint lives of the participant and the spouse, and
which is the actuarial equivalent of a single annuity for the life of the participant.

children.heading: Definition of qualified preretirement survivor annuity
children.label: c Definition of qualified preretirement survivor annuity
i
children.text: in the case of a participant who dies after the date on which the participant attained the earliest retirement age, such participant had retired with an immediate qualified joint and survivor annuity on the day before the participant’s date of death, or

children.label: I
children.text: separated from service on the date of death,

children.label: II
children.text: survived to the earliest retirement age,

children.label: III
children.text: retired with an immediate qualified joint and survivor annuity at the earliest retirement age, and

children.label: IV
children.text: died on the day after the day on which such participant would have attained the earliest retirement age, and

children.label: B
children.text: under the plan, the earliest period for which the surviving spouse may receive a payment under such annuity is not later than the month in which the participant would have attained the earliest retirement age under the plan.

children.heading: Special rule for defined contribution plans
children.label: 2 Special rule for defined contribution plans
children.text: In the case of any defined contribution plan or participant described in clause (ii) or (iii) of section 401(a)(11)(B), the term “qualified preretirement survivor annuity” means an annuity for the life of the surviving spouse the actuarial equivalent of which is not less than 50 percent of the portion of the account balance of the participant (as of the date of death) to which the participant had a nonforfeitable right (within the meaning of section 411(a)).

children.heading: Security interests taken into account
children.label: 3 Security interests taken into account
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (1) and (2), any security interest held by the plan by reason of a loan outstanding to the participant shall be taken into account in determining the amount of the qualified preretirement survivor annuity.

children.heading: Survivor annuities need not be provided if participant and spouse married less than 1 year
children.label: d Survivor annuities need not be provided if participant and spouse married less than 1 year
A
children.text: the participant’s annuity starting date, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date of the participant’s death.

children.heading: Treatment of certain marriages within 1 year of annuity starting date for purposes of qualified joint and survivor annuities
children.label: 2 Treatment of certain marriages within 1 year of annuity starting date for purposes of qualified joint and survivor annuities
A
children.text: a participant marries within 1 year before the annuity starting date, and

children.label: B
children.text: the participant and the participant’s spouse in such marriage have been married for at least a 1-year period ending on or before the date of the participant’s death,

children.heading: Plan may require distribution if present value not in excess of dollar limit
children.label: 1 Plan may require distribution if present value not in excess of dollar limit
children.text: A plan may provide that the present value of a qualified joint and survivor annuity or a qualified preretirement survivor annuity will be immediately distributed if such value does not exceed the amount that can be distributed without the participant’s consent under section 411(a)(11). No distribution may be made under the preceding sentence after the annuity starting date unless the participant and the spouse of the participant (or where the participant has died, the surviving spouse) consents in writing to such distribution.

children.heading: Plan may distribute benefit in excess of dollar limit only with consent
children.label: 2 Plan may distribute benefit in excess of dollar limit only with consent
A
children.text: the present value of the qualified joint and survivor annuity or the qualified preretirement survivor annuity exceeds the amount that can be distributed without the participant’s consent under section 411(a)(11), and

children.label: B
children.text: the participant and the spouse of the participant (or where the participant has died, the surviving spouse) consent in writing to the distribution,

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraphs (1) and (2), the present value shall not be less than the present value calculated by using the applicable mortality table and the applicable interest rate.

children.heading: Applicable mortality table
children.label: B Applicable mortality table
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “applicable mortality table” means a mortality table, modified as appropriate by the Secretary, based on the mortality table specified for the plan year under subparagraph (A) of section 430(h)(3) (without regard to subparagraph (C) or (D) of such section).

children.heading: Applicable interest rate
children.label: C Applicable interest rate
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “applicable interest rate” means the adjusted first, second, and third segment rates applied under rules similar to the rules of section 430(h)(2)(C) (determined by not taking into account any adjustment under clause (iv) thereof) for the month before the date of the distribution or such other time as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe.

children.heading: Applicable segment rates
children.label: D Applicable segment rates
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (C), the adjusted first, second, and third segment rates are the first, second, and third segment rates which would be determined under section 430(h)(2)(C) (determined by not taking into account any adjustment under clause (iv) thereof) if section 430(h)(2)(D) were applied by substituting the average yields for the month described in subparagraph (C) for the average yields for the 24-month period described in such section.

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: f Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Vested participant
children.label: 1 Vested participant
children.text: The term “vested participant” means any participant who has a nonforfeitable right (within the meaning of section 411(a)) to any portion of such participant’s accrued benefit.

children.label: i
children.text: the first day of the first period for which an amount is payable as an annuity, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a benefit not payable in the form of an annuity, the first day on which all events have occurred which entitle the participant to such benefit.

children.heading: Special rule for disability benefits
children.label: B Special rule for disability benefits
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the first day of the first period for which a benefit is to be received by reason of disability shall be treated as the annuity starting date only if such benefit is not an auxiliary benefit.

children.heading: Earliest retirement age
children.label: 3 Earliest retirement age
children.text: The term “earliest retirement age” means the earliest date on which, under the plan, the participant could elect to receive retirement benefits.

children.heading: Plan may take into account increased costs
children.label: 4 Plan may take into account increased costs
children.text: A plan may take into account in any equitable manner (as determined by the Secretary) any increased costs resulting from providing a qualified joint or survivor annuity or a qualified preretirement survivor annuity.

children.heading: Distributions by reason of security interests
children.label: 5 Distributions by reason of security interests
children.text: If the use of any participant’s accrued benefit (or any portion thereof) as security for a loan meets the requirements of subsection (a)(4), nothing in this section or section 411(a)(11) shall prevent any distribution required by reason of a failure to comply with the terms of such loan.

children.heading: Requirements for certain spousal consents
children.label: 6 Requirements for certain spousal consents
children.text: No consent of a spouse shall be effective for purposes of subsection (e)(1) or (e)(2) (as the case may be) unless requirements comparable to the requirements for spousal consent to an election under subsection (a)(1)(A) are met.

children.heading: Consultation with the Secretary of Labor
children.label: 7 Consultation with the Secretary of Labor
children.text: In prescribing regulations under this section and section 401(a)(11), the Secretary shall consult with the Secretary of Labor.

children.heading: Definition of qualified optional survivor annuity
children.label: g Definition of qualified optional survivor annuity
A
children.text: for the life of the participant with a survivor annuity for the life of the spouse which is equal to the applicable percentage of the amount of the annuity which is payable during the joint lives of the participant and the spouse, and

children.label: B
children.text: which is the actuarial equivalent of a single annuity for the life of the participant.

children.label: i
children.text: is less than 75 percent, the applicable percentage is 75 percent, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is greater than or equal to 75 percent, the applicable percentage is 50 percent.

children.heading: Survivor annuity percentage
children.label: B Survivor annuity percentage
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “survivor annuity percentage” means the percentage which the survivor annuity under the plan’s qualified joint and survivor annuity bears to the annuity payable during the joint lives of the participant and the spouse.
